qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Missing ownership validation in annotation layer, theme, and tag deletion enables unauthorized data modification
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding targets object-level ownership validation for annotation layers, themes, and tags, not the advisory-described dataset creation/update path where overwriting an existing dataset SQL query bypasses data access controls.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: CSRF-exempt chart data endpoint enables cross-origin SQL query execution via victim's browser session
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding targets CSRF exemption and cookie-authenticated POST handling for chart data query execution, not the advisory-described improper authorization flaw in dataset SQL overwrite checks.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described Apache Superset root cause: an authenticated user with dataset write and chart read permissions can bypass data access controls by overwriting the SQL query of an existing dataset during dataset creation or update. The qualifying findings concern different code paths and security checks.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-3m2g-v7jf-7fxc / CVE-2026-23982 describes improper authorization in Apache Superset before 6.0.0 where dataset permission checks can be bypassed by overwriting an existing dataset SQL query.

authorization_bypass_annotation_layer/vulnerability_report.json reports missing ownership checks in annotation layer, theme, and tag update/delete commands, with entrypoints such as DELETE /api/v1/annotation_layer/<pk> and PUT /api/v1/theme/<pk>.
csrf_chart_data_query_execution/vulnerability_report.json reports CSRF-exempt chart data and explore_json endpoints accepting cookie-authenticated POST requests without a CSRF token.

Neither qualifying report mentions dataset creation, dataset update, existing dataset SQL query overwrite, dataset data access permission checks, or the advisory's source-to-sink/control-flow condition.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding exists, so Tier1, Tier2, and Tier3 are not applicable.
